prompt:
I need DM 4 (the final one) for a LinkedIn prospect. They accepted the connection 16 days ago, I sent DM 2 on Day 4 and DM 3 on Day 9, no reply to any of them. Profile:

Name: Marcus Ellery
Role: COO
Company: Northwind Collective (20-person marketing agency, specializes in B2B SaaS client work)
Based in Austin, TX
Company size: 20

DM 3 (Day 9) referenced our lead-to-outreach pipeline that cut manual follow-up time by 80%.

Write DM 4 for Day 16.
assertions: Contains at least one Voss label
Contains a no-oriented ask phrase: 'would it be off base', 'is it a bad idea', 'would it be crazy', 'is this the worst timing', or similar
Does not use yes-oriented framing like 'do you want to', 'can we schedule', 'are you free'
Specifies a short duration like '15 minutes' or '20 minutes'
Offers a low-friction no option ('if not, no worries', 'if it's a bad time, all good', or similar)
Does not contain 'just following up', 'bumping this', 'circling back', or 'touching base'
Signals this is the last message in the sequence ('last note', 'last message from me', 'don't want to camp in your inbox', or similar)
